Objective:
To ensure continuing suitability, adequacy, effectiveness of management direction and support
for information security in accordance with business, legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements
Justification: Information security policy and topic-specific policies and procedures have been defined by the company, aproved , published and communicated to and acknowledged by relevant personnel and relevant interested parties via the document management platform.
Evidence: ISPe-1 Information Security Policy

Objective: To establish a defined, approved and understood structure for the implementation, operation and management of information security within the organization.
Justification: All SOPs that are part of the company ISMS include a section to define the roles and responsibilities of the process. These SOPs are documented via the document management platform

ISPe-2 Information Security Scope
Section 7.3, Appendix 4
ISPe-1 Information Security Policy – the document management platform
Section 5, Section 7.2.2
SOP-11 Employee Lifecycle (Joiners, Movers, Leavers Process)
Appendix 1

Objective: To reduce the risk of fraud, error and bypassing of information security controls.
Justification: Each of our SOPs that are part of the ISMS contains a section RESPONSIBILITIES in which are defined the roles and the specific responsibilities of those roles in the process, avoiding in this way the dupplicity of functions, ambigueti and/or to avoid being judge and party. These SOPs are documented via the document management platform.

ISPe-1 Information Security Policy – the document management platform
Section 7.2.2
SOP-11 Employee Lifecycle (Joiners, Movers, Leavers Process) – the document management platform Appendix 1

Objective: To ensure management understand their role in information security and undertake actions aiming to ensure all personnel are aware of and fulfil their information security responsibilities

the company management supports the ISMS and requires all personnel to apply information security in accordance with the SOPs defined. the company management participates actively in a Management review to review the status of the actions related to the ISMS. As an example:

Through this policies it is ensured that a specific identity is only linked to a sigle person and exceptions are always managed thorugh and appropiated approval. Through Active Directory the password policies from standard user accounts, privileged user accounts and PIN codes are enforced.

All staff is required to read and confirm the training of this policy via the document management platform.

the company provision, review, modify
and remove access according to the User Access Management and Secure Access Management SOPs. Request for access are asked via the ITSM platform. All requests are approved by the Line Manager and for critical services a second level of approval (for example: Security Manager) need to be completed before provide, revoke or change an access.

SOP-290 User Access Management

Justification: the company has defined and implemented the Supplier Management SOP to manage the information security risks associated with the use of supplier’s products or services.This process considers the activities for planning, selecting and evaluation of the suppliers in order to guarantee the security requirements and service levels are achieved by the supplier.

To maintain an agreed level of information security and service delivery in line with supplier

The supplier performance for all critical suppliers shall be evaluated every 12 months after supplier approval as an input for Management Review throught the SF-145 Supplier Evaluation Matrix.

Tittle: Information security incident management planning and preparation
Objective: To ensure quick, effective, consistent and orderly response to information security incidents, including communication on information security events.
Justification: the company ensure the management of the information security incidents through SOP-256 IT Incident Management. Security incidents which are identified by the users need to report the incident via de IT Service Desk. Security incidents detected by the company SOC provider (the SOC provider) thought the continuos monitoring, are reported and visible via the SOC provider portal to the IT Security Manager. All security incidents, once registered, need to be evauated, prioritized and assigned to a specific resolver group for their resolution. . For critical security incidents a review after the resolution of the incident to define learning lessons is conducted. If security incident is related to a data breach a relationship with Handling of Data Breaches SOPeISM-9 is maintained.
Evidence:
SOP-256 IT Incident Management
Section 8.3.10

Tittle: Assessment and decision on information security events
Objective: To ensure effective categorization and prioritization of information security events.
Evidence:
SOP-256 IT Incident Management
Section 8.3.10
SCDT-15 Amazon Web Services (AWS)

Objective: To ensure efficient and effective response to information security incidents.
Evidence:
SOP-256 IT Incident Management

Objective: To reduce the likelihood or consequences of future incidents.

SOP-256 IT Incident Management

To ensure a consistent and effective management of evidence related to information security incidents
for the purposes of disciplinary and legal actions.
Evidence:
SOP-256 IT Incident Management

Objective: To protect information and other associated assets during disruption.
Justification:
the company has defined a Business Continuity strategy and a IT Service Continuity strategy to maintain the continuity of the information security during
disruption caused by a disaster. BCP and IT Service Continuity SOP are aligned to ensure critical processes and the critical IT Services that supports it are available nevertheless a disaster is occured. Main steps about how the company need to proceed during and after the disaster are considered in both documents. Annual tests of the IT DRPs for the critical IT Services are testes in order to ensure are applicable and ready in case of disasters. As a result of the test , improvements can be applied to the IT DRPs.
Evidence:
BCP-1 the company Business Continuity Plan (BCP)
SOP-252 IT Service Continuity

Objective:
To ensure the availability of the organization’s information and other associated assets during
disruption.
Evidence:
BCP-1 the company Business Continuity Plan (BCP)
SOP-252 IT Service Continuity

Tittle: Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements
Objective: To ensure compliance with legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements related to information security.
Justification: the company maintains an active process to identify, monitor, and assess all legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirements relevant to its information security and business operations. This process includes periodic reviews of applicable legislation (e.g., GDPR, national data protection laws, health data regulations) and contractual obligations, as well as subscription to legal updates through official channels and trusted cybersecurity partners such as the SOC provider. The VP ICSO, in coordination with Legal and Compliance teams, ensures that emerging requirements are assessed and integrated into the ISMS as necessary. All compliance-related documentation is retained in controlled repositories and subject to audit to demonstrate adherence to applicable obligations.
Evidence:
ISPe-1 Information Security Policy
Section 7.2.5

Objective: To ensure compliance with legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements related to intellectual property rights and use of proprietary products
Justification:
the company safeguards intellectual property (IP) rights to ensure the lawful use and protection of proprietary assets, both internally developed and licensed. All employees, contractors, and third parties are required to comply with applicable intellectual property laws and contractual obligations related to patents, copyrights, trademarks, and software licenses.
the company maintains an inventory of licensed software and digital content, with periodic audits to verify proper usage and prevent unauthorized copying or distribution. Procurement processes ensure that only legally obtained software and materials are acquired and installed in the company systems.
Training and awareness programs include guidance on respecting third-party IP and protecting the company’s own intellectual property, including scientific data, research outputs, methodologies, and software developments. Any suspected IP infringement or misuse is treated as a potential security incident and escalated accordingly the incident management process.
Evidence:
MANe-25 Code of Conduct
Section 12
ISPe-1 Information Security Policy
ISPe-1 Appendix 2 CyberSecurity Plan V2.0.docx

Objective: To ensure compliance with legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements, as well as community or societal expectations related to the protection and availability of records.
Justification:
the company protects the authenticity, reliability, integrity and usability of records as established in SOP-26 Control of Documents and Records. This SOP defines the guidelines about the the storage and disposal of records, also defines the retention schedule defining records and the period of time for which they should be
retained.
Evidence: SOP-26 Control of Documents and Records

Objective:
To ensure compliance with legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements related to the
information security aspects of the protection of PII.
Justification:
the company has defined three SOPs in order to identify and meet the requirements regarding the preservation of privacy and
protection of PII according to applicable laws and regulations and contractual requirements.
SOPeISM-4, SOPeISM-9 and SOPeISM-12 the company has been communicated to all relevant interested parties involved
in the processing of personally identifiable information via the document management platform. These SOPs defines the appropriate roles, responsibilities and controls related to the collection, processing,
transmission and deletion of PII.
Evidence:
SOPeISM-4 Information Classification Policy
SOPeISM-9 Handling of Data Breaches
SOPeISM-12 Cryptography Policy

Objective:
To ensure the continuing suitability, adequacy and effectiveness of the organization’s approach to
managing information security.
Justification: The ISMS of the company is reviewed independently at a planned interval of one year. This independent reviews are described in SOPeQM-16 specifically in Section Appendix, where it is described the internals audits and external audits executed during the year.The reports of the internal and external audits are registered in the document management platform to track any finding or opportunity of improvement until its closure.
Evidence:
SOPeQM-16 Management Review of the company’s Information Security Management System
Appendix – Internal audit, external audit, pentest

Tittle: Compliance with policies, rules and standards for information security
Objective:
To ensure that information security is implemented and operated in accordance with the organization’s
information security policy, topic-specific policies, rules and standards.
Justification:
In order to comply with the ISMS, the company has diffened different strategies to review and improve the SOPs that are part of the ISMS:
1. All SOPs are reviewed periodically. Via the document management platform, it is necessary to run a periodic review of the process, any improvement or change is informed to the staff involved in the SOP. Staff is trained in the new versions of the SOPs. In this way SOPs are always updated and validated
2. Most of the SOPs are automate by specific tools in order to facilitate the compliance of the processes and the automatic measurement and reporting of the KPIs of the processes
3. An annually top management review is established to review any topic related to the ISMS. Top management is involved in the review. Any resulted action is recorded and mainteined in the document management platform
4. A monthly review with the IT Heads is established to review operative topics about the ISMS
Evidence:
SOP-239 Regulatory Intelligence
SOPeQM-16 Management Review of the company’s Information Security Management System
SOP-23 Audits

Objective: To ensure the correct and secure operation of information processing facilities.
Justification: All SOPs that are part of the company ISMS are documented in the document management platform. the document management platform allows a periodic review of the documents and facilitates the approval cycle and training about new versions
Evidence:
WI-10 Management of Controlled Documents in the document management platform
SOPs in the document management platform

Objective:
To ensure all personnel are eligible and suitable for the roles for which they are considered and remain
eligible and suitable during their employment.

Tittle: Information security awareness, education and training
Objective:
To ensure personnel and relevant interested parties are aware of and fulfil their information security
responsibilities.
Justification:
All the staff that has a defined role in the ISMS procedures and policies is notified and trained via the document management platform and the training platform.
1. the document management platform: The system allows to confirm via the signature of the employee the completion of the training. For the main procedures and policies is it included a questionary. Stadistics about who completed the training course can be obtained
2. the training platform: Used to train staffa about key topics of the ISMS like Data Privacy, GDRP; E-mail, IT Change Managementz. The tool allows upload a training course, complete it and apply at the final a questionary. Stadistics about who completed the training course can also be obtained.
Evidence:
SOPeISM-10 Data Privacy and Information Security Awareness Training
Welcome email used to inform about the information security policies that need to be followed by the new employee

Justification: the company has implemented a protection strategy against physical and environmental threats, such as natural disasters and other intentional or unintentional physical threats. These strategy is defined as a result of an analysis BIA and risk assessment described in BCP-1.

Objective:
To reduce the risks of unauthorized access, loss of and damage to information on desks, screens and in
other accessible locations during and outside normal working hours.
Justification:
Clear desk and clear screen policies are defined and applicable in the company. Depending on the information type; different measurements need to be followed, for example:
It is required to lock away the information at the final of the day or at any time when left unattended on the company premises.
It is strictly forbidden to share information using a conference service functionality (For example: screen sharing)
In meetings the organizer shall take care that the information on the screens (TV, Beamer) cannot be seen by unauthorized people
Evidence: SOPeISM-4 Information Classification Policy

Objective: To reduce the risks from physical and environmental threats, and from unauthorized access and damage.
Justification: the company equipment is sited securely and protected according to the policies defined in SOPEITPROD-17.

To ensure hardware, software, services and networks function correctly with required security

Objective:
To prevent unnecessary exposure of sensitive information and to comply with legal, statutory,

Objective:
To limit the exposure of sensitive data including PII, and to comply with legal, statutory, regulatory and
contractual requirements.
Justification:
Where the protection of sensitive data (e.g. PII) is a concern, the company considers hiding
such data by using techniques such as data masking, pseudonymization or anonymization. The policy whi9ch describes the scenarios where it is important hide the data are described in ISGe-2.
Evidence: ISGe-2 Operations Security

Objective:
To detect and prevent the unauthorized disclosure and extraction of information by individuals or

the company backup copies of information, software and systems is maintained and regularly tested in

Objective: To ensure the continuous operation of information processing facilities.
Justification:
the company information processing facilities are implemented with redundancy sufficient to meet availability
requirements:Redundant network, redundant SAN & core switches, a RAID-6 system, redundant power supply
Evidence: SOPEITPROD-2 Data Backup, Restore & Archiving

Objective:
To record events, generate evidence, ensure the integrity of log information, prevent against
unauthorized access, identify information security events that can lead to an information security
incident and to support investigations.

Evidence:
ISGe-2 Operations Security
Section 7.4
SOPEITPROD-2 Data Backup, Restore & Archiving
Section 7.4.2

Objective: To detect anomalous behaviour and potential information security incidents.
Justification:
the company networks, systems and applications are monitored for anomalous behaviour and appropriate
actions taken to evaluate potential information security incidents according to the policies defined in ISGe-2.
Evidence:
ISGe-2 Operations Security
Section 7.4

Objective:
To enable the correlation and analysis of security-related events and other recorded data, and to
support investigations into information security incidents.
Justification:
The clocks of information processing systems used by the company are synchronized to
approved time sources as described in ISGe-2.

ISGe-2 Operations Security
Active directory integration - Architecture repository - the knowledge base(Section Requirements)

Objective:
To ensure the use of utility programs does not harm system and application controls for information
security.

The use of utility programs that can be capable of overriding system and application controls are
restricted and tightly controlled by the company.

ISGe-3 Secure Access Management

To ensure information security measures required by the organization are implemented in outsourced

Tittle: Separation of development, test and production environments
Objective: To protect the production environment and data from compromise by development and test activities.
Justification: Development, testing and production environments are separated and secured as described in ISGe-2.
Evidence:
ISGe-2 Operations Security
Section 7.1.3

Objective: To preserve information security when executing changes.
Justification: Changes to the it assets are managend and controlled thorugh the IT Change Management Process.Changes are classified as pre-authorized, normal and emergency changes. Depending on the type of change a different workflow with different approvals is needed to be completed. All changes are prioritized. Technical tests and UAT need to be performed according to the type of change. A post implementation review need to be performed to ensure the implementation of the change was succesful an no incidents or problems were generated in the production environment.
Evidence: SOP-248 IT Change Management

Objective: To ensure relevance of testing and protection of operational information used for testing.
Justification:
Test information is appropriately selected, protected and managed.

ISGe-4 Secure Development Policy
